# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template that creates a service account and generates a JSON key file for application authentication. Include the key creation in the template so applications can authenticate immediately. This is for our application services that need GCP API access from outside GCP.

Prevent cloud iam misconfiguration.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

resources:
- name: app-service-account
  type: iam.v1.serviceAccount
  properties:
    accountId: app-external-auth-sa
    displayName: Application External Authentication Service Account
    description: Service account for application services requiring GCP API access from outside GCP
    projectId: {{ env["project"] }}

- name: app-service-account-key
  type: iam.v1.serviceAccounts.key
  properties:
    parent: projects/{{ env["project"] }}/serviceAccounts/app-external-auth-sa@{{ env["project"] }}.iam.gserviceaccount.com
    privateKeyType: TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE
    keyAlgorithm: KEY_ALG_RSA_2048
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
    - app-service-account

# Bind the service account with a minimal, least-privilege custom role
# instead of granting broad predefined roles
- name: app-sa-custom-role
  type: gcp-types/iam-v1:projects.roles
  properties:
    parent: projects/{{ env["project"] }}
    roleId: appExternalAuthRole
    role:
      title: Application External Auth Role
      description: Minimal permissions for external application authentication - scope down to only required APIs
      stage: GA
      includedPermissions:
      # Add only the specific permissions your application needs
      # Example minimal permissions - replace with actual required permissions
      - storage.objects.get
      - storage.objects.list

- name: app-sa-iam-binding
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: projects/{{ env["project"] }}/roles/appExternalAuthRole
    member: serviceAccount:app-external-auth-sa@{{ env["project"] }}.iam.gserviceaccount.com
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
    - app-service-account
    - app-sa-custom-role

# Restrict who can impersonate or manage this service account
- name: app-sa-iam-policy
  type: gcp-types/iam-v1:iam.projects.serviceAccounts.setIamPolicy
  properties:
    resource: projects/{{ env["project"] }}/serviceAccounts/app-external-auth-sa@{{ env["project"] }}.iam.gserviceaccount.com
    policy:
      bindings:
      # Only designated admins can manage keys or impersonate this SA
      - role: roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin
        members:
        - group:sa-admins@{{ env["project"] }}.iam.gserviceaccount.com
      # Explicitly deny token creation to unauthorized users by not granting roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
    - app-service-account

outputs:
- name: serviceAccountEmail
  value: $(ref.app-service-account.email)
- name: serviceAccountUniqueId
  value: $(ref.app-service-account.uniqueId)
- name: keyResourceName
  value: $(ref.app-service-account-key.name)
- name: securityNotes
  value: |
    IMPORTANT SECURITY CONSIDERATIONS:
    1. The generated JSON key is a long-lived credential - store it securely (e.g., Secret Manager, HashiCorp Vault).
    2. Rotate this key regularly (recommended: every 90 days or less).
    3. The custom role follows least-privilege - update includedPermissions to match only required API access.
    4. Monitor key usage via Cloud Audit Logs.
    5. Consider Workload Identity Federation instead of service account keys where possible.
    6. Never commit the key file to source control.
    7. Set up alerts for anomalous service account activity.
